description:
## Parent

Parent epic: `bsm-dbh` — Filtering and search issue list views.

## Background

The Issue Explorer already contains a search field with the placeholder `Search issues...` and a visual Cmd+F hint, but the input is disabled and does not affect rows. The feature needs local Issue Search that is fast, predictable, and intentionally scoped to loaded Beadwork data rather than a server-side grep or Beadwork command.

This first search slice wires the search box and search algorithm for the default All Issues view only. That provides a small, testable foundation before applying the same query to every active Issue List View in a later task.

## What to build

Enable the Issue Search input after a successful load and make it narrow the All Issues view. Search should inspect only:

- Issue ID
- Issue title
- raw Issue description

Do not include comments, labels, rendered Markdown text, dependency IDs, status, priority, type, assignee, or other metadata in this slice unless explicitly called out above.

The search behavior should be:

- Case-insensitive.
- Query is trimmed and split on whitespace into tokens.
- Every token is required (AND semantics).
- Tokens match by substring, so partial tokens progressively narrow results.
- Punctuation inside a token is preserved, so `bsm-123` works naturally.
- Empty or whitespace-only search has no tokens and does not narrow All Issues.
- Results preserve the All Issues base order.
- Search updates immediately as the user types; do not debounce.

## Technical Approach

Add a small pure search helper or equivalent well-tested logic. Normalize searchable text from `issue.id`, `issue.title`, and `issue.description`. Keep raw descriptions as raw strings; do not depend on rendered Markdown or comments.

The search query should be local React state and should not persist across app reloads. Keep the existing placeholder `Search issues...` and the existing Cmd+F visual hint. Custom Cmd+F/Ctrl+F keyboard behavior remains out of scope.

Search should be enabled for any successful load, including a true-empty workspace or a currently empty All result. It should remain disabled during loading and failure.

## Considerations

- This task intentionally handles All Issues only. Later work will apply the same query to Ready, Blocked, and status views.
- Counts in the sidebar must remain base counts and should not change as search text changes.
- Search-result highlighting is out of scope.
- Avoid overfitting tests to helper names; test visible behavior and algorithm semantics.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Search input is enabled after successful load and disabled during loading/failure.
- [ ] Typing in the search box immediately narrows the All Issues rows.
- [ ] Search covers Issue ID, title, and raw description only.
- [ ] Search is case-insensitive, whitespace-tokenized, AND-based, substring-based, and punctuation-preserving.
- [ ] Empty or whitespace-only search does not narrow All Issues.
- [ ] Search preserves All Issues order and does not sort matches.
- [ ] Search query is not persisted across app reloads.
- [ ] The placeholder remains `Search issues...` and the Cmd+F visual hint remains visible.

## Test Plan

- Pure helper tests for tokenization, case-insensitivity, AND semantics, partial token matching, punctuation-preserving Issue ID searches, and whitespace-only queries.
- Component tests proving search filters All rows by ID, title, and description but not comments.
- Component tests proving search preserves row order.
- Component tests for enabled/disabled search states.
- Component tests proving sidebar counts do not change while typing.
title: Add All-only local Issue Search foundation
